behavior: An agile aerial insectivore, catching small flying insects on the wing. Often forages in flocks; outside the breeding season it may gather in large communal roosts. Nests in cavities or nest boxes.
field_marks: Iridescent blue-green head and upperparts in adults
Clean white throat, breast, and belly
Thin dark mask through the eye
Long, pointed wings with blackish flight feathers
Short tail, squared to slightly notched
habitat: Open country near water, including fields, marshes, shorelines, ponds, and wooded swamps; requires cavities or nest boxes for nesting.
